concerns.text: T007 defines adaptive sampling as 'Target N=500, fallback to N=200 if runtime > 4 hours'. This violates FR-011 which mandates sampling to ≤500 subjects ONLY if the 6-hour CPU limit is exceeded. The task introduces a 4-hour fallback threshold not authorized by the spec, silently weakening the data retention requirement and altering the experimental design.

T035 implements 'permutation testing (up to 10,000 permutations, adaptive to CPU limit)'. While it mentions adaptivity, it fails to explicitly encode the hard constraint from FR-007 (up to 10,000) vs the Plan's feasibility reality (1,000). More critically, without an explicit task to enforce the 6-hour hard cap (FR-011) as the trigger for reduction, this task risks implementing the Plan's '1,000' limit as a default rather than a fallback, violating the spec's requirement for maximum statistical power up to the limit.

T029 documents the Yeo-7 definition in 'results/reports/methodology.md'. FR-014 requires the SYSTEM to document this to avoid circular bias. While T029 covers the content, the spec implies this is a system-level validation/documentation step. However, the more critical issue is T037/T042 which generate the final report. The task list lacks a specific verification step ensuring the final report (FR-008/FR-014) explicitly states the associational nature and Yeo-7 definition as a system-generated assertion, not just a manual note.

T038 computes robustness check stability but lacks the verification step required by SC-003. SC-003 requires measuring stability against a target of ≥80% consistency. T038 only says 'Compare effect direction consistency'. It does not include a task to validate, log, or fail the build if the 80% threshold is not met, effectively dropping the acceptance criterion of the success metric.

T041 'Validate power analysis' is a one-time check. SC-005 requires sample size power to be measured against ≥80% power for r=0.25. The task does not specify that this validation must be automated and re-run if the dataset is sampled (e.g., if N drops to 200 per FR-011). If the system samples to 200, the power drops below 80%. The task list lacks a mechanism to re-evaluate or flag this condition dynamically, violating the continuous nature of the success criterion.
